content:
Hello everyone! I’m new here and interested in the Rust for Linux project. I’d like to take on some good first issues, but unfortunately, it seems that the ones on GitHub have already been claimed. I was wondering if there might be other suitable tasks that could be assigned to me. This is my first experience with Linux kernel development, so I’m not very familiar with the kernel yet. I’m hoping this opportunity will help me gain a deeper understanding of the Linux kernel.

Thank you!

wenzhao said:

I was wondering if there might be other suitable tasks that could be assigned to me.

It depends on what you are looking for :wink: If you like to start not with a large development task, but rather with some learning about patch submission you might want to have a look to the # Ok::<(), Error>(()) topic. That would be one patch for the dox() replacement topic and one patch for the .unwrap() replacement. For .unwrap() replacement you could do even more patches as we have that in some other files, as well.

Just in case you like to pick this, for the commit message of the dox() patch you could use 

...

Link: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/459782fe-afca-4fe6-8ffb-ba7c7886de0a@de.bosch.com/
Suggested-by: Miguel Ojeda <ojeda@kernel.org>
Signed-off-by: Dirk Behme <dirk.behme@de.bosch.com>
Signed-off-by: <your SOB>

Dirk Behme said:

you might want to have a look to the # Ok::<(), Error>(()) topic.

Just for the logs, @Daniel Sedlak is working on this topic. Thanks!
Is this topic still valid? I'm seeking for new issues for beginners but github issues are all taken.

i was actually looking at this tag today after a discussion, so i did some stats.

There are currently 18 open good first issues. Out of those 18, 1 is the reminder to go look at contribution how-to, 8 are waiting for either a new version of a patchset or a first patchset (1122, 1121, 1119, 1116, 1110, 1109, 1006, 351), and all others have a patch set sent that is currently either awaiting review or where no update was given in the issue (1075, 1104, 1125, 1128, 1131, 1132, 1138, 1139, 1140).

That is to say, there are currently no available good first issues.
